title: Lasiorrhiza philippiana
taxonomy: Lasiorrhiza philippiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae. This family is commonly known as the moonseed family. The genus Lasiorrhiza is a small group of plants native to the Philippines. Lasiorrhiza philippiana is native to the island of Luzon in the Philippines, where it grows in the wild. It is a climber that uses its long stems to support itself in its native habitat.
uses: Lasiorrhiza philippiana has been traditionally used in folk medicine in the Philippines for its medicinal properties. Its roots and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
